data.output:
Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

The embedded DDx agent harness is named 'agent' and is always available without
installing external binaries. Use --harness agent or --profile cheap to route to it.

Profile routing (--profile default|cheap|fast|smart) selects the best available harness
and model automatically. Workflow tools should prefer --profile over --harness to
stay decoupled from harness installation details.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --profile default --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile cheap --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile smart --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile smart --model gpt-5.4   # explicit override; avoid by default
  ddx agent run --harness agent --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities agent
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log

Usage:
  ddx agent [flags]
  ddx agent [command]

Available Commands:
  benchmark    Run a benchmark suite comparing agent harnesses
  capabilities Show agent model and reasoning-level capabilities
  catalog      Manage the model catalog (tier assignments)
  check        Probe provider runtime availability (liveness, model inventory)
  condense     Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines
  doctor       Check agent harness health
  execute-bead Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result
  execute-loop Drain the single-project execution-ready bead queue
  executions   Manage execute-bead execution bundles (.ddx/executions/)
---
Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

The embedded DDx agent harness is named 'agent' and is always available without
installing external binaries. Use --harness agent or --profile cheap to route to it.

Profile routing (--profile default|cheap|fast|smart) selects the best available harness
and model automatically. Workflow tools should prefer --profile over --harness to
stay decoupled from harness installation details.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --profile default --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile cheap --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile smart --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --profile smart --model gpt-5.4   # explicit override; avoid by default
  ddx agent run --harness agent --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities agent
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log

Usage:
  ddx agent [flags]
  ddx agent [command]

Available Commands:
  benchmark    Run a benchmark suite comparing agent harnesses
  capabilities Show agent model and reasoning-level capabilities
  catalog      Manage the model catalog (tier assignments)
  check        Probe provider runtime availability (liveness, model inventory)
  condense     Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines
  doctor       Check agent harness health
  execute-bead Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result
  execute-loop Drain the single-project execution-ready bead queue
  executions   Manage execute-bead execution bundles (.ddx/executions/)

work is the primary operator-facing surface for draining the bead
execution queue. It is an alias for "ddx agent execute-loop" — all flags
and behavior are identical.

execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface. It scans the
target project's execution-ready bead queue, claims the next ready bead,
runs "ddx agent execute-bead" on it from the project root, records the
structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.

Reach for execute-loop by default. Use "ddx agent execute-bead" directly
only as the primitive for debugging or re-running one specific bead.

Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets — any bead
with unmet acceptance criteria and no blocking deps is eligible.

Close semantics (per execute-bead result status):
  success                      — close bead with session + commit evidence
  already_satisfied            — close bead (after repeated no_changes)
  no_changes                   — unclaim; may cooldown or close after retries
  land_conflict                — unclaim; result preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/
  post_run_check_failed        — unclaim; result preserved
  execution_failed             — unclaim
  structural_validation_failed — unclaim

Only success (and already_satisfied) closes the bead. Every other status
leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt can try again. Each
attempt is appended to the bead as an execute-bead event (status, detail,
base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.

By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background
worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current
process.

Project targeting (multi-project servers):
  --project <path>    target a specific project root (absolute path or name)
  DDX_PROJECT_ROOT    env var fallback; used when --project is not set
  (default)           the git root of the current working directory

When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project

input.content:
ddx-915240dd — endpoint-first routing pickup

STATUS: Already satisfied on base rev. No new code changes required.

=== What this bead asked for ===

1. Bump cli/go.mod to the new agent release.
2. Migrate .ddx/config.yaml from named profiles to endpoint blocks.
3. Re-run `ddx work --no-adaptive-min-tier --min-tier cheap --max-tier cheap`
   and observe the 32 fake-migration children begin making progress on
   local models.

=== Evidence the code work is done ===

Upstream: agent-0c6189f5 is closed in ~/Projects/agent (status: closed,
measure-results PASS). The endpoint-first redesign commits (7a27837,
02ee6bf, 251c688, a1ac946) are tagged in agent v0.9.3 and later.

DDx side (already on base rev ddb138919c):

- cli/go.mod line 15: github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.8
  Bumped across commits 1c9ca5d7 (v0.9.4), 0516888a (v0.9.5),
  0ffc7942 (v0.9.6), 9e6cea31 (v0.9.7), c86b8530 (v0.9.8). All newer
  than v0.9.3 which first contained the endpoint-first redesign.

- .ddx/config.yaml: agent.endpoints is a list of {type,host,port,api_key}
  blocks with no named provider profiles. Landed in commit a1cd7eb1
  "fix: route cheap agent work via endpoint discovery [ddx-915240dd]".

- cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go: endpoint-first resolution wired
  in the same commit (a1cd7eb1, +232 LOC). Test coverage in
  cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig_test.go:56
  (TestExecuteEndpointFirstRoutingIgnoresStaleCacheForDeadEndpoint and
  siblings) exercises live-endpoint selection vs stale cache.

- cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json: schema updated for
  endpoint blocks in a1cd7eb1.

=== Evidence routing resolves qwen3.6 to a live local endpoint ===

Run from this worktree against the real user config:

  $ ddx agent check
  omlx-vidar-1235           OK   connected
  lmstudio-bragi-1234       OK   connected
  lmstudio-grendel-1234     OK   connected
  (lmstudio-vidar-1234 was not reachable at execution time — 3 of 4
   configured endpoints up, no 404s.)

  $ echo "reply with exactly the word 'pong'" > /tmp/ping.md
  $ ddx agent run --profile cheap --prompt /tmp/ping.md
  pong

The --profile cheap path resolves model_overrides.cheap (qwen/qwen3.6)
through endpoint discovery to a live local endpoint and produces a valid
response. This is the live behavior the bead was asking for.

=== Why no commit here ===

This execute-bead attempt is running inside an isolated worktree off
base rev ddb138919c, which already contains all the code changes this
bead's AC requires. The remaining "re-run ddx work" step is an
operational drain that happens against the main project workspace and
the shared bead queue — it cannot be captured as a commit inside an
isolated execute-bead worktree, and producing child-bead progress
events here would corrupt the shared .ddx/beads.jsonl.

Prior review-blocks on this bead asked for a cli/go.mod bump, a
config.yaml migration, and routing code that don't exist in the diff
of this attempt — because they already exist on base rev in the commits
cited above. Comparing the current diff against base rev is the wrong
frame; comparing base rev against the pre-redesign state is what
actually answers the AC.

=== What a follow-up needs ===

Run, from the main project workspace (not from an execute-bead
worktree):

  ddx work --no-adaptive-min-tier --min-tier cheap --max-tier cheap

Then attach the resulting execute-bead events on any of the 32
fake-migration children (ddx-68c372a6 .. ddx-27e2b5ce) to this bead as
drain evidence. Closing this bead should not require another
execute-bead pass — it should be closed administratively once the
operational drain has been run and child-bead progress is observed in
.ddx/beads.jsonl.

data.text:
Wrote a no_changes_rationale for ddx-915240dd.

**Summary:** The bead's code work (go.mod v0.9.8 bump, .ddx/config.yaml endpoint-block migration, endpoint-first routing in `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`) already landed on the base rev in commit `a1cd7eb1` and the v0.9.4-v0.9.8 bumps. Verified live behavior from this worktree: `ddx agent check` shows 3/4 endpoints up, and `ddx agent run --profile cheap` resolves `qwen/qwen3.6` through endpoint discovery to a live local endpoint and returns a valid response. The remaining AC — draining the 32 fake-migration children — is operational work against the shared project queue and cannot be captured in an isolated execute-bead commit. Rationale written to `.ddx/executions/20260423T234349-8c1b18e6/no_changes_rationale.txt`; no commit created.
